creator: dar@thekeep.org
text:
After rebuilding the world and kernel, ppp now fails to connect to ISP
(Earthlink).  No configuration changes were made to ppp.conf.  The only
modification to any configuration was the addition of option LIBICONV to
the kernel config for smbfs.  The previous world/kernel build was from
2001-10-16.  Windows ME running on the same hardware combination continues
to work correctly, so the change does not appear to be at the ISP level.

Removing LIBICONV does not appear to change the problem.  Nor does removing
the ipfw/bridge/dummynet kernel options, which were also MFC'd during the
period between a working and non-working ppp.  Using a ppp binary built on
a -STABLE system as of mid-Setember also does not work.  

Below are two examples of the syslog PPP output indicating the failure.
Note, in particular, that MRRU is being forced for negotiation for some
reason, despite not having a multilink setup.  However, while 'set mrru
1524' (the requested value) eliminates the conflict there, the
'<0x0>[4] 0000' rejection conflict remains.

As another point of interest, during the same period, the original chat
script, with a rather lengthy modem init string started to fail, while a
simpler init string works.  Perhaps a problem in the sio(4) driver?

ppp.conf contains the following:

#################################################################
# $FreeBSD: src/etc/ppp/ppp.conf,v 1.2.2.5 2001/07/13 10:55:23 brian Exp $
#################################################################

default:
 set log Phase LCP IPCP #HDLC
 # set log Chat CCP tun command
 ident user-ppp VERSION (built COMPILATIONDATE)
 set server /tmp/pppctl foo 0007

 # Ensure that "device" references the correct serial port
 # for your modem. (cuaa0 = COM1, cuaa1 = COM2)
 #
 set device /dev/cuaa0

 set speed 115200
 set dial "ABORT BUSY ABORT NO\\sCARRIER TIMEOUT 5 \
           \"\" AT OK-AT-OK ATE1Q0 OK \\dATDT\\T TIMEOUT 40 CONNECT"
 set timeout 180			# 3 minute idle timer (the default)
 enable dns				# request DNS info (for resolv.conf)

papchap:
 #
 # edit the next three lines and replace the items in caps with
 # the values which have been assigned by your ISP.
 #

 set phone  XXXXXXX
 set authname ELN/XXXXXXXXXX
 set authkey XXXXXXX

 set ifaddr 10.0.0.1/0 10.0.0.2/0 255.255.255.0 0.0.0.0
 add default HISADDR			# Add a (sticky) default route
 # set mrru 1524			# Earthlink is suddenly demanding this?!

machine	i386

ident	ANKOU

/etc/sysctl.conf
# $FreeBSD: src/etc/sysctl.conf,v 1.1.2.1 2000/07/27 22:59:35 obrien Exp $
#
#  This file is read when going to multi-user and its contents piped thru
#  ``sysctl -w'' to adjust kernel values.  ``man 5 sysctl.conf'' for details.
#
kern.ipc.shmmax=67108864
kern.ipc.shmall=32768
net.inet.tcp.always_keepalive=1
vfs.vmiodirenable=1
kern.ipc.shm_use_phys=1

/boot/loader.conf

hw.ata.wc="1"
hw.ata.tags="0"
hw.ata.ata_dma="1"
hw.ata.atapi_dma="1"
snd_maestro3_load="YES"
agp_load="YES"
r128_load="YES"
